title: ProseMirror anchor resolution with checksum validation
purpose: Provides utilities to create and resolve position-resilient anchors for annotations that can recover from document changes using checksums and text scanning.
module_context: Exports helper functions for computing checksums and creating/resolving anchors in ProseMirror documents.

steps.content: Initialize a 32-bit hash value with a fixed seed.
For each character in the input text, XOR the hash with the character code.
Multiply the hash by a 32-bit FNV prime using integer multiplication.
Convert the final hash to an 8-character hexadecimal string with zero padding.

methods.name: createAnchor
methods.normalized_params: (doc: ProseMirrorNode, from: number, to: number): Annotation['anchor']

steps.content: Extract the text content between the given positions in the document.
Compute a checksum for the extracted text.
Return an anchor object containing the positions, text, and checksum.

methods.name: resolveAnchor
methods.normalized_params: (doc: ProseMirrorNode, anchor: Annotation['anchor']): { from: number; to: number } | null

steps.content: Validate the cached range coordinates against document boundaries.
IF the cached range is valid, extract the text and verify its checksum.
IF the checksum matches, return the cached range.
ELSE scan the full document text for the anchor text.
IF the text is not found, return null.
ELSE map the string offset back to ProseMirror positions using text node traversal.
Return the resolved positions or null if mapping fails.
Initialize tracking variables for consumed text length and result position.
Walk through document nodes to accumulate text lengths and locate the target offset.
For textblock nodes, account for block separators when calculating position.
For text nodes, advance the consumed length and check if the target offset is reached.
For leaf nodes, treat them as single-character separators and advance the consumed length.
IF the target offset exactly matches the consumed length at document end, return the document size.
Return the calculated position or null if not found.
